Section 2e – Other exemptions The applicant confirms that one of the following other grounds for
exemption applies:
Please provide any additional
the application would be made without notice (Paragraph 5.1 of Practice
information requested in the
Direction 18A sets out the circumstances in which applications may be made
text box on page 8.
without notice.)
a child is one of the prospective parties.
(i) the prospective applicant is not able to attend a MIAM online or by
video-link and an explanation of why this is the case is provided to the
court using the text box on page 8; and
(ii) the prospective applicant has contacted as many authorised family
mediators as have an office within fifteen miles of his or her home (or
five of them if there are five or more), and all of them have stated that
they are not available to conduct a MIAM within fifteen business days of
the date of contact; and
(iii) the names, postal addresses and telephone numbers or e-mail
addresses for the authorised family mediators contacted by the
prospective applicant, and the dates of contact, are provided to the
court in the text box on page 8.

metadata.title: Confirm if you have attended a Mediation Information and Assessment Meeting: Form FM1
metadata.description: Use this form to confirm to the court if you have attended a Mediation Information and Assessment Meeting. If you have not, you must give the reason why.
form_fields: Child protection concerns you must complete section 2b
Domestic violence you must complete section 2a
